finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_02.md:11 now reads 'Twelve years of marriage had made them experts at the precise weight of what went unsaid.' chapters/chapter_02.md:87 reads 'Twelve years of marriage compressed into twenty minutes and a blue button and a case number.' Wedding date June 14, 2017 (chapters/chapter_02.md:67) to early March 2029 is approximately 11 years 9 months; 'twelve years' is within the 6-month rounding margin. Both references now agree.
finding_results.notes: Changed line 11 from 'fourteen years' to 'twelve years' to match both line 87's existing 'twelve years' and the arithmetic of June 2017 to March 2029. No other marriage-duration references exist in the chapter.

finding_results.evidence: All five proxy-tenure references now read 'eighteen months': chapters/chapter_02.md:3 ('Eighteen months ago'), chapters/chapter_02.md:15 ('every morning for eighteen months'), chapters/chapter_02.md:23 ('Eighteen months of the same coffee'), chapters/chapter_02.md:45 ('eighteen months trying to describe it'), chapters/chapter_02.md:121 ('eighteen months of proxy life'). These match the continuity sheet's canonical 'about eighteen months.'
finding_results.notes: Changed all five instances from 'fourteen months' to 'eighteen months.' The finding identified three locations; two additional instances at lines 45 and 121 were also updated for internal consistency.

finding_results.evidence: Three scene-level sensory moments now interrupt the analytical stretch: (1) chapters/chapter_02.md:27 — 'Her coffee had gone cold by page six' (temperature/touch detail grounding the briefing review in time and body); (2) chapters/chapter_02.md:39 — 'pressed her palms flat against its cold surface' (touch detail anchoring the post-presentation reflection); (3) chapters/chapter_02.md:43 — 'Her jaw ached. She unclenched it, feeling the muscles release along the hinge — she'd been holding her teeth together through the entire presentation without knowing it' (body sensation after the declarative pivot 'She used to run things'). The language metaphor at chapters/chapter_02.md:45 is now 2 sentences of metaphor content, down from 4+.
finding_results.notes: Added three physical/sensory interruptions (acceptance test required 2+). Compressed the language metaphor by removing the exhaustive elaboration ('You haven't lost the fluency. The words are still there...') and the 'party trick — impressive, decorative, and useless' closer, folding the core image into a single dash-joined sentence.

finding_results.evidence: Two physical/visceral responses beyond 'hands gone still' now appear: (1) chapters/chapter_02.md:111 — 'Her throat had gone tight' at the point of first comprehension, between the third reading and the italicized terms; (2) chapters/chapter_02.md:121 — 'the cold prickling up the back of her neck' at the recognition of systemic opacity. The violent/physical metaphor appears at chapters/chapter_02.md:121: 'something had reached into her kitchen and closed its fist around her divorce, and she couldn't see the fist, couldn't find the angle to pry it off.' This metaphor uses Mira's signature 'angle' vocabulary and renders the threat as physical intrusion consistent with her stress profile.
finding_results.notes: The radiator's tick was also reworked from 'suddenly precise and countable' to 'arrived like something being counted against her' to sharpen the paranoid/threatened register. The original ending ('This was the thing that had no phone number, no human face, no angle she could find') was replaced with the fist metaphor, which preserves the 'no angle' stress tell while adding the violent/physical edge the acceptance test required.

finding_results.evidence: Tricolon count reduced from 15+ to 8. Remaining tricolons at chapters/chapter_02.md:13 (lobby echo), chapters/chapter_02.md:25 (data/analysis/judgment), chapters/chapter_02.md:39 (haircut/blazer/face), chapters/chapter_02.md:57 (data/operations/audience), chapters/chapter_02.md:77 (Click sequence), chapters/chapter_02.md:115 (audience/user/asset), chapters/chapter_02.md:121 (Not proxy/Not divorce/This), chapters/chapter_02.md:133 (burn mark/coffee ring/view). No two tricolons share a paragraph. Converted to pairs at chapters/chapter_02.md:23, chapters/chapter_02.md:27, chapters/chapter_02.md:35, chapters/chapter_02.md:45, chapters/chapter_02.md:47, chapters/chapter_02.md:49, chapters/chapter_02.md:67, chapters/chapter_02.md:69, chapters/chapter_02.md:85, chapters/chapter_02.md:87, chapters/chapter_02.md:91, chapters/chapter_02.md:119, chapters/chapter_02.md:123, chapters/chapter_02.md:131, chapters/chapter_02.md:135, chapters/chapter_02.md:139. Quartets retained at chapters/chapter_02.md:47 (face/voice/handshake/willingness) and chapters/chapter_02.md:137 (apartment/finances/routine/schedule).
finding_results.notes: All conversions preserve semantic content. Pair constructions use 'and' connectors or dash-appositives. Strongest tricolons retained: the Click sequence (comic timing), the data/operations/audience mirror pair (lines 25 and 57), and the identity cascade (line 115). Rhythmic variety substantially improved across the chapter.

finding_results.evidence: Exactly 2 instances of 'the way [subject] [verb]' remain: chapters/chapter_02.md:9 ('the way a child carries a blanket') and chapters/chapter_02.md:131 ('the way children sleep when they still believe the floor beneath them is solid'). These are 122 lines apart. Removed instances: line 15 changed to 'like Theo had'; line 21 changed to 'like a filing cabinet'; line 69 changed to 'how Dan had looked at her'; line 111 recast as 'with the close attention she'd once given competitor filings'; line 119 recast as '*OK*, the same syllable he'd use for a delayed train'; line 129 recast as 'just as she knew the transit notifications weren't sent for her benefit'.
finding_results.notes: The two retained instances are the chapter's strongest: the blanket simile (line 9) establishes Mira's relationship to her pre-machine identity, and the children-sleeping simile (line 131) closes the chapter's emotional arc. Replacements use varied constructions (like, how, with, just as, direct recast) to avoid creating a new formulaic pattern.

finding_results.evidence: 'She was [identity noun]' appears 4 times: chapters/chapter_02.md:29 ('She was a face attached to an intelligence'), chapters/chapter_02.md:33 ('She was good at this'), chapters/chapter_02.md:115 ('She was, apparently, the asset'), chapters/chapter_02.md:137 ('she was a woman who'd been trained to find the angle'). One tricolon-identity cascade retained at chapters/chapter_02.md:115 ('She wasn't the audience. She wasn't the user. She was, apparently, the asset.'). The former cascade at line 129 restructured as appositives with dash: 'A flag on a petition, a line in a queue — something the system would get to.' Lines 115 and 129 are now structurally distinct: 115 uses full subject-verb sentences with anaphora; 129 uses noun-phrase appositives with a dash pivot.
finding_results.notes: The retained cascade at line 115 earns its repetition — it arrives at 'the asset,' the chapter's sharpest insight about Mira's reclassification. The restructured line 129 preserves the diminution through appositives rather than identity declarations, creating a different rhythmic and syntactic shape.

finding_results.evidence: Zero instances of explicit prose self-annotation remain. Removed at chapters/chapter_02.md:9: cut 'and she was aware of the analogy, and she did not care' — the blanket simile now ends cleanly. Compressed at chapters/chapter_02.md:23: 'the consistency should have been comforting, and it was not comforting, and the distance between *should have been* and *was not* was the exact territory her days occupied' replaced with 'the consistency should have been comforting, and it was not.' — the gap registers through the truncation itself. Removed at chapters/chapter_02.md:49: cut 'which at this point amounted to the same thing' from 'meaning it, or seeming to mean it' — the comma-or carries the collapse of distinction without annotation.
finding_results.notes: All three metacommentary instances removed. The blanket simile is stronger without the self-aware gloss. The coffee-routine passage gains force from the abrupt 'and it was not' — the truncation enacts the emptiness rather than analyzing it. The handshake passage lets 'or seeming to mean it' sit without explanation, trusting the reader to feel the corrosion. The chapter's interiority remains deep (Mira is still visibly analytical) but now the analysis emerges through her actions — note-taking, protocol-reading, form-filing — rather than through the prose commenting on its own images.

pass_summaries.summary: All four findings resolved. Two HIGH continuity defects fixed: marriage duration reconciled to 'twelve years' across both references (matching June 2017 wedding to March 2029 story date), and proxy tenure aligned to 'eighteen months' across all five instances (matching the continuity sheet). Two MEDIUM craft defects fixed: the analytical run in lines 25-47 now contains three sensory interruptions and a compressed metaphor; the notification discovery section now contains two visceral responses beyond the original 'hands gone still' plus a violent/physical metaphor consistent with Mira's stress profile. No new continuity contradictions introduced. Heading contract preserved. No sanitization, no elliptical clipping, no style-bible example lines echoed.

All four findings addressed. Tricolon count reduced from 15+ to exactly 8 (max 8 required), with no two in the same paragraph and 16+ non-three-item constructions providing rhythmic variety. 'The way [subject] [verb]' reduced from 7 to exactly 2 instances, 122 lines apart. 'She was [identity noun]' reduced to 4 instances with one cascade retained and the second restructured into a distinct syntactic shape. All three metacommentary instances removed. Revisions preserve the chapter's tonal register (controlled then alarming), Mira's voice signatures (strategic vocabulary, analytical interiority, physical stress tells), and continuity details (2/3 train, Bergen Street, Gemini-state Financial District, canvas tote, oak table, burn mark, June 14 2017 wedding, Theo age 9, third-floor Park Slope brownstone). No content softened or sanitized; the chapter's raw material — professional humiliation, marital grief, bureaucratic entrapment — retains full specificity. Heading contract preserved.
